item.title: This is a test on using VSC to write blogs
item.description: Funny that it works. Funny that it works.

item.title: Undefined and control flow in Javascript (why you see undefined when typing let x = 3 at console) ?
item.description: Question When you type let x = 3 (declare a variable) in a console of Chrome/Firefox/… often you get a mysterious undefined. It’s the same story when you type function f(){} (function declaration). The explanation can be quite intimidating as this answer here. Here is a simple (well not sooo simple) answer. Completion type and control flow In Javascript, the Completion type is a Record used to explain the runtime propagation of values and control flow such as the behaviour of statements (break, continue, return and throw, normal) that perform nonlocal transfers of control.
